ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hunting_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hunting unit */,
  "hunt_number" TEXT /* Hunt number associated with the unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name/description of the hunting unit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the hunting area */,
  "terrain_type" TEXT /* Type of terrain in the hunting unit */,
  "wildlife_population" TEXT /* Information about wildlife population in the unit */,
  "area_size" REAL /* Size of the hunting unit in square kilometers */,
  "accessibility" TEXT /* Information on how to access the hunting unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maps" (
  "map_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each map */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hunting unit */,
  "static_pdf" TEXT /* File path to the static PDF map */,
  "custom_map" TEXT /* Instructions or functionality for creating a custom map */,
  "map_type" TEXT /* Type of map (e.g., topographic, satellite) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the map was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the map was last updated */,
  "map_creator" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the map */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maps_map_creator FOREIGN KEY ("map_creator") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maps_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hunting_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login purposes */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., hunter, guide, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hunting_activities" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the activity */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hunting unit where the activity occurred */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the activity */,
  "actions" TEXT /* Description of actions taken (e.g., hunting, scouting) */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., hunting, scouting, observation) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the activity in hours */,
  "wildlife_sighted" TEXT /* Types of wildlife sighted during the activity */,
  "compliance_status" INTEGER /* Whether the activity was compliant with regulations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hunting_activities_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hunting_activities_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hunting_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regulations" (
  "regulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each regulation */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated hunting unit */,
  "regulation_text" TEXT /* Text describing the specific regulation for the unit */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the regulation becomes effective */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Date when the regulation expires */,
  "regulation_type" TEXT /* Type of regulation (e.g., seasonal, permanent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("regulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regulations_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hunting_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the permission applies */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action that the user can perform */,
  "granted" INTEGER /* Whether the permission is granted (true/false) */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "granted_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who granted the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_granted_by FOREIGN KEY ("granted_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
